target.name: (-)-Harmicine
target.motivation: Tetrahydro-beta-carboline skeletons are prominent and ubiquitous in an extraordinary range of indole alkaloid natural products and pharmaceutical compounds. Powerful synthetic approaches for stereoselective synthesis of tetrahydro-beta-carboline skeletons have immense impacts and have attracted enormous attention. (-)-Harmicine exhibited antileishmanial and antinociceptive activities.

success_criteria.free_text: Efficient collective assembly of complex natural products with architectural and stereochemical divergence from a general method remains highly desirable. The method should enable access to 40 THβC-type indole alkaloid natural products belonging to five families and enantiodivergent total syntheses of eburnane-alkaloids.

key_disconnections.reasoning: The N-O bond of isoxazolidine conferred structural advantages that could be cleaved by assorted alkyl halides to induce additional functionality and complexity that would be difficult to be achieved from traditional approaches.

This reaction was hypothesized to be a possible solution to synthesize THβC derivatives with continuous multi-chiral centers in one-step, synthesize 1,1-disubstituted THβCs possessing C1-chiral quaternary carbon center enantioselectively, and apply the methods to collective syntheses of natural products and bioactive molecules.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The strategy involves a catalytic enantioselective 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide with vinyl ether to form tetrahydro-beta-carboline fused isoxazolidines. These isoxazolidines then undergo N-O bond cleavage and further functionalization to access various THβC-type indole alkaloids. This approach aims to address challenges in synthesizing THβC derivatives with continuous multi-chiral centers and quaternary stereocenters, and to enable collective and enantiodivergent total syntheses of natural products.

thought.background: Existing methods for enantioselective synthesis of THβCs face challenges in synthesizing derivatives with continuous multi-chiral centers in one step, synthesizing 1,1-disubstituted THβCs with C1-chiral quaternary carbon centers enantioselectively, and applying methods to collective syntheses of natural products.
thought.gap: A unified platform starting material is needed to construct quaternary stereocenters and undergo further transformation for a large collection of natural product syntheses.
thought.decision: To address these challenges, we hypothesized that 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide, a type of nitrone containing DHβC skeleton, represented an attractive platform molecule to synthesize THβC-type structural motif. Taking advantage of nitrone chemistry, 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide has been reported to be reactive in 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with dipolarphiles. We wished to develop the asymmetric conversion of 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxides and explore their application in natural product syntheses.

outcome.observation: Hypothesis formed to use 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide in asymmetric 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition for THβC synthesis.

evidence.text_span: To address these challenges, we hypothesized that a possible solution might arise from investigation of unified platform starting materials for their ability to construct quaternary stereocenters and undergo further transformation for a large collection of natural product syntheses. We considered that 3,4-dihydro-·β-carboline-2- oxide (Fig. 2), a type of nitrone containing DHβC skeleton, represented an attractive platform molecule to synthesize THβC-type structural motif. Taking advantage of nitrone chemistry, 3,4-dihydro-β-carboline-2-oxide has been reported to be reactive in 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with dipolarphiles55–61. However, the 3,4-dihydro-·β- carboline-2-oxide chemistry was unexplored in enantioselective form. Towards this end, we wished to develop the asymmetric conversion of 3,4-dihydro ·β-carboline-2-oxides and explore their application in natural product syntheses.

thought.background: A general strategy for asymmetric synthesis of THβCs is desired.
thought.gap: Optimal conditions for chiral phosphoric acid catalyzed 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide with vinyl ether need to be identified.
thought.decision: To test the hypothesis, preliminary studies began with the investigation of chiral phosphoric acid catalyzed 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide (1a) with vinyl ether (2a) in DCM at -20 °C using BINOL-derived chiral phosphoric acid 4a.

outcome.observation: The desired tetrahydro-beta-carboline fused isoxazolidine 3a was obtained in 37% yield with 5:1 dr and 50% ee.

evidence.text_span: To test our hypothesis, the preliminary studies began with the investigation of chiral phosphoric acid64–74 catalyzed 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of 3,4-dihydro-β-carboline-2-oxide with vinyl ether (Table 1). 3,4- Dihydro-·β-carboline-2-oxide 1a and vinyl ether 2a were chosen as initial substrates. The reaction catalyzed by BINOL-derived chiral phosphoric acid 4a was first conducted in DCM at −20 °C. The desired tetrahydro-β-carboline fused isoxazolidine 3a was obtained in 37% yield with 5:1 dr and 50% ee (entry 1).

thought.background: Initial reaction in DCM gave 37% yield, 5:1 dr, and 50% ee.
thought.gap: The yield, diastereoselectivity, and enantioselectivity need to be improved.
thought.decision: To improve the reaction, different solvents were screened. CHCl3 was found to be the best solvent.

outcome.observation: CHCl3 afforded 3a with moderate diastereoselectivity (7:1 dr) and enantioselectivity (73% ee).

evidence.text_span: Then, different solvents were screened (entries 1–6), and CHCl3 (entry 2) was found to be the best solvent, affording 3a with moderate diastereoselectivity (7:1 dr) and enantioselectivity (73% ee).

thought.background: Reaction in CHCl3 gave 42% yield, 7:1 dr, and 73% ee.
thought.decision: To improve enantioselectivity, molecular sieves were added to the reaction in CHCl3.

outcome.observation: The ee value was improved to 81% with the addition of 3 Å MS.

evidence.text_span: Molecular sieves were proved to be beneficial for the improvement of enantioselectivity (entries 7–9), and the ee value was improved to 81% with the addition of 3 Å MS (entry 7).

thought.background: Reaction with 3 Å MS in CHCl3 gave 42% yield, 8:1 dr, and 81% ee.
thought.gap: Yield needs to be dramatically enhanced without deteriorating stereoselectivity.
thought.decision: To enhance the yield, various chiral phosphoric acids were screened. BINOL-derived CPA 4b was used as catalyst.

outcome.observation: The yield of 3a was dramatically enhanced to 71% without deterioration of diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity when BINOL-derived CPA 4b was used as catalyst.

evidence.text_span: Encouraged by this promising result, various chiral phosphoric acids were screened. To our delight, the yield of 3a was dramatically enhanced without deterioration of diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity when BINOL-derived CPA 4b was used as catalyst (entry 10).

thought.background: Reaction with CPA 4b in CHCl3 with 3 Å MS gave 71% yield, 8:1 dr, and 81% ee.
thought.gap: Further improvement in diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity is desired.
thought.decision: To improve stereoselectivity, the reaction temperature was lowered to -40 °C.

outcome.observation: Lowering the reaction temperature to -40 °C improved the yield to 81%, dr to 10:1, and ee to 94%.

evidence.text_span: Then, it is indicated that lower reaction temperature was better for the diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity. Overall, systematic evaluation of the reaction variables such as solvent, additive, catalyst, and reaction temperature identified the optimal reaction condition shown in entry 16, which gave 3a in 85% yield with >19:1 dr and 98% ee.

thought.background: Reaction at -40 °C gave 81% yield, 10:1 dr, and 94% ee.
thought.gap: Further optimization of reaction temperature for optimal stereoselectivity.
thought.decision: To achieve optimal conditions, the reaction temperature was further lowered to -60 °C.

outcome.observation: The optimal reaction condition was identified, giving 3a in 85% yield with >19:1 dr and 98% ee.

evidence.text_span: Then, it is indicated that lower reaction temperature was better for the diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity. Overall, systematic evaluation of the reaction variables such as solvent, additive, catalyst, and reaction temperature identified the optimal reaction condition shown in entry 16, which gave 3a in 85% yield with >19:1 dr and 98% ee.

thought.background: The optimal conditions for the cycloaddition of 1a and 2a were established (85% yield, >19:1 dr, 98% ee).
thought.gap: The scalability of the protocol needs to be demonstrated.
thought.decision: To demonstrate scalability, a 5 mmol scale experiment of 1a with 2a was performed under standard reaction conditions.

outcome.observation: The gram-scale reaction smoothly provided 3a in 82% yield with >19:1 dr and 98% ee.

evidence.text_span: We also demonstrated the scalability of this protocol by a 5 mmol scale experiment of 1a with 2a under standard reaction condition. The gram-scale reaction smoothly provided 3a in 82% yield with >19:1 dr and 98% ee.

thought.background: The catalytic enantioselective 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of 3,4-dihydro-beta-carboline-2-oxide with vinyl ether is established.
thought.gap: The synthetic utility and versatility of the method need to be showcased through post-functionalization of the isoxazolidine products.
thought.decision: To showcase synthetic utility, N-O bond cleavage of 3a was performed using BnBr, followed by Boc protection of the indole N-H to obtain ester 10.

outcome.observation: Ester 10 was obtained in 95% yield by a one-pot process.

evidence.text_span: As shown in Fig. 6, N–O bond cleavage of 3a by using BnBr and Boc protection of indole N–H gave ester 10 in 95% yield by one-pot process.

thought.gap: Further transformation of ester 10 is needed to advance towards natural product synthesis.
thought.decision: To advance the synthesis, ester 10 underwent DIBAL-H reduction followed by reaction with Wittig reagent and hydrolysis with HCO2H/DCM to afford aldehyde 11.

outcome.observation: Aldehyde 11 was obtained in 50% yield over two steps.

evidence.text_span: Subsequent DIBAL-H reduction followed by reaction with Wittig reagent and hydrolysis with HCO2H/DCM afforded aldehyde 11 in 50% yield over two steps

thought.gap: Further transformation is needed to obtain the final natural product.
thought.decision: To obtain the final product, aldehyde 11 underwent a sequential deprotection/reduction to obtain compound 12.

evidence.text_span: which underwent a sequential deprotection/ reduction to obtain compound 12 in 76% yield.

thought.gap: The Boc group needs to be removed to yield the natural product.
thought.decision: To obtain the natural product, the Boc group was removed from compound 12.

outcome.observation: Natural product (-)-harmicine 13 was obtained in 91% yield.

evidence.text_span: Removal of Boc group gave natural product (−)-harmicine 1379 in 91% yield, which exhibited antileishmanial and antinociceptive activities80 (Fig. 6a).

validation.significance: The developed catalytic enantioselective 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition provides a highly efficient protocol toward three types of chiral tetrahydro-beta-carboline fused isoxazolidines generating two stereocenters, three continuous stereocenters, and quaternary stereocenters in high yields with excellent diastereoselectivities and enantioselectivities. The method enabled collective syntheses of 40 THβC-type indole alkaloid natural products belonging to five families and enantiodivergent total synthesis, thus facilitating the synthesis efficiency.

source.title: Catalytic enantioselective nitrone cycloadditions enabling collective syntheses of indole alkaloids.
source.authors: Tian X, Xuan T, Gao J, Zhang X, Liu T, Luo F, Pang R, Shao P, Yang YF, Wang Y.
